content.text: Answer four problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

problem.text: Give an example of each of the following, with some explanation.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: A group \(G\) and a subgroup \(H \leq G\) such that \(C_G(H) \neq N_G(H)\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: A group \(G\) and a subgroup \(H \leq G\) such that \(H \nsubseteq C_G(H)\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: A group \(G\) and a normal subgroup \(H \trianglelefteq G\) such that \(C_G(H) \neq G\).

problem.text: Let \(G\) be a group and let \(T_G = \{x \in G : x^n = 1 \text{ for some } n \geq 1\}\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(G\) is abelian then \(T_G\) is a subgroup of \(G\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example of a nonabelian group \(G\) such that \(T_G\) is not a subgroup of \(G\). Justify any claims that you make.

problem.text: Let \(G\) be a finite group which acts on the set \(S\). Let \(H\) be a normal subgroup of \(G\) with the following property: For every \(x_1, x_2 \in S\), there is a unique \(h \in H\) such that \(h(x_1) = x_2\). Choose \(x \in S\) and let \(G_x = \{g \in G : g(x) = x\}\) denote the stabilizer of \(x\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that if \(H \leq Z(G)\) then \(G_x \trianglelefteq G\) and \(G\) is the internal direct product of \(G_x\) and \(H\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that every simple subgroup of \(S_4\) is abelian.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Use the result from (a) to prove that if \(G\) is a nonabelian simple group then every proper subgroup of \(G\) has index at least 5.

problem.text: Let \(G\) be a group of order \(76 = 4 \cdot 19\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that \(G\) contains a normal Sylow 19-subgroup.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that the center of \(G\) contains an element of order 2.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define the derived series \(G^{(0)} \geq G^{(1)} \geq G^{(2)} \geq \cdots\) of \(G\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that \(G\) is solvable if and only if \(G^{(n)} = \{1\}\) for some \(n \geq 0\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Give an example of a solvable group \(G\) such that \(G^{(1)} \neq \{1\}\).
